approved.descriptionHtml: A fine-grained personal access token request was approved.
approved.summaryHtml:
This event occurs when there is activity relating to a request for a fine-grained personal access token to access resources that belong to a resource owner that requires approval for token access. For more information, see "Creating a personal access token."

To subscribe to this event, a GitHub App must have at least read-level access for the "Personal access token requests" organization permission.

bodyParameters.name: personal_access_token_request
bodyParameters.description: Details of a Personal Access Token Request.

childParamsGroups.name: id
childParamsGroups.description: Unique identifier of the request for access via fine-grained personal access token. Used as the pat_request_id parameter in the list and review API calls.

childParamsGroups.name: owner
childParamsGroups.description: A GitHub user.

childParamsGroups.name: permissions_added
childParamsGroups.description: New requested permissions, categorized by type of permission.

childParamsGroups.name: permissions_upgraded
childParamsGroups.description: Requested permissions that elevate access for a previously approved request for access, categorized by type of permission.

childParamsGroups.name: permissions_result
childParamsGroups.description: Permissions requested, categorized by type of permission. This field incorporates permissions_added and permissions_upgraded.

childParamsGroups.name: repository_selection
childParamsGroups.description: Type of repository selection requested.

childParamsGroups.name: repository_count
childParamsGroups.description: The number of repositories the token is requesting access to. This field is only populated when repository_selection is subset.

childParamsGroups.name: repositories
childParamsGroups.description: An array of repository objects the token is requesting access to. This field is only populated when repository_selection is subset.

childParamsGroups.name: id
childParamsGroups.description: Unique identifier of the repository

childParamsGroups.name: name
childParamsGroups.description: The name of the repository.

childParamsGroups.name: private
childParamsGroups.description: Whether the repository is private or public.

childParamsGroups.name: created_at
childParamsGroups.description: Date and time when the request for access was created.

childParamsGroups.name: token_id
childParamsGroups.description: Unique identifier of the user's token. This field can also be found in audit log events and the organization's settings for their PAT grants.

childParamsGroups.name: token_name
childParamsGroups.description: The name given to the user's token. This field can also be found in an organization's settings page for Active Tokens.

childParamsGroups.name: token_expired
childParamsGroups.description: Whether the associated fine-grained personal access token has expired.

childParamsGroups.name: token_expires_at
childParamsGroups.description: Date and time when the associated fine-grained personal access token expires.

childParamsGroups.name: token_last_used_at
childParamsGroups.description: Date and time when the associated fine-grained personal access token was last used for authentication.

bodyParameters.name: enterprise
bodyParameters.description: An enterprise on GitHub. Webhook payloads contain the enterprise property when the webhook is configured on an enterprise account or an organization that's part of an enterprise account. For more information, see "About enterprise accounts."

bodyParameters.name: organization
bodyParameters.description: A GitHub organization. Webhook payloads contain the organization property when the webhook is configured for an organization, or when the event occurs from activity in a repository owned by an organization.

bodyParameters.name: sender
bodyParameters.description: A GitHub user.

bodyParameters.name: installation
bodyParameters.description: The GitHub App installation. Webhook payloads contain the installation property when the event is configured for and sent to a GitHub App. For more information, see "Using webhooks with GitHub Apps."

cancelled.descriptionHtml: A fine-grained personal access token request was cancelled by the requester.
